title: Add repository-scoped ball projection
goal: Expose a read-only current-ball projection per repository resource and allow next-task explanation and claiming to be filtered by repository resource without introducing a second queue canon.

metadata.source_request: User requested one current ball per repository after queue canon made registry/queue.json dispatch-canonical.
metadata.safety_boundary: Repository-scoped balls are projection and selection filters only; they do not create a second queue, auto-promote tasks, relax reservations, or grant merge/completion authority.
metadata.outcome: Implemented in Bureau PR #247: repository-scoped ball projection via repo-balls plus --resource filters for frontier, explain-next, claim-next and checkout-next. registry/queue.json remains the dispatch canon; repository balls are read-only projection/selection filters only.
validation: Bureau PR #247 GitHub CI passed validate (3.10) and validate (3.12).
Local before merge: python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.
Local before merge: python3 -m bureau.cli --root . --json doctor -> healthy true.
Local before merge: python3 -m bureau.cli --root . --json registry-truth -> healthy true, 0 findings.
Local before merge: python3 -m bureau.cli --root . --json repo-balls --capability repository --capability shell -> projection emitted repository balls.
Local before merge: python3 -m pytest -> 378 passed.
Local before merge: python3 -m ruff check . -> passed.
Local before merge: git diff --check -> passed.
Post-merge: python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.

verified_acceptance.repo-ball-projection: tests/test_v2.py covers repo_balls projecting current balls for repo.alpha and repo.beta; post-merge live repo-balls emitted a repository projection.
verified_acceptance.resource-filtered-explain: tests/test_v2.py covers explain_next(resource=repo.beta) excluding non-overlapping repo.alpha tasks.
verified_acceptance.resource-filtered-claim: tests/test_v2.py covers claim_next(resource=repo.alpha/repo.beta) selecting overlapping task claims while preserving Dispatcher.reasons.
verified_acceptance.worker-boundary: tests/test_v2.py covers stable scoped worker IDs and rejects reuse of an active worker for a different resource filter.
verified_acceptance.docs-boundary: docs/concurrency.md and docs/operations.md state repository balls do not replace registry/queue.json and do not auto-promote lanes.

acceptance.assertion: A read-only command reports one current ball per repo.* resource using active runs, eligible tasks and queued lane state.
explain-next/frontier can be scoped to a repository resource and exclude tasks that do not claim overlapping resources.
claim-next/checkout-next can be scoped to a repository resource while preserving reservation, capability, lifecycle and open-PR gates.
The one-active-assignment-per-worker invariant is preserved; parallel repository balls require distinct stable worker IDs.
Documentation states that repository-scoped balls do not replace registry/queue.json as dispatch canon and do not auto-promote lanes.
